heading: Disposition of deposits of coal, and so forth
label: 193 Disposition of deposits of coal, and so forth
text: The deposits of coal, phosphate, sodium, potassium, oil, oil shale, and gas, herein referred to, in lands valuable for such minerals, including lands and deposits in Lander, Wyoming, coal entries numbered 18 to 49, inclusive, shall be subject to disposition only in the form and manner provided in this chapter, except as provided in sections 1716 and 1719 of title 43, and except as to valid claims existent on , and thereafter maintained in compliance with the laws under which initiated, which claims may be perfected under such laws, including discovery.February 25, 1920
notes: CodificationSection was from act , in which words now reading “in Lander, Wyoming, coal entries numbered 18 to 49, inclusive,” originally read “described in the joint resolution entitled ‘Joint resolution authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to permit the continuation of coal mining operations on certain lands in Wyoming,’ approved , (Thirty-seven Statutes at Large p. 1346).” The change was effected by interpolation, in lieu of the reference to the 1912 resolution, the actual description of lands contained in said resolution.Feb. 25, 1920August 12, 1912

Amendments1978— provided for disposition of minerals as provided in sections 1716 and 1719 of title 43.Pub. L. 95–5541946—Act , excluded from section 5 of act , the incorporation, by reference, of , and reenacted inclusion of deposits of potassium.Aug. 8, 1946Feb. 7, 1927section 181 of this title1927—Act , included deposits of potassium.Feb. 7, 1927
